message:
franken_numpy-nys5 add polynomial basis conversion helper wrappers

Add eight numpy.polynomial.<family> passthrough wrappers to fnp-python
that were missing per POLYNOMIAL_COMPLIANCE.generated.md, covering the
four non-HermiteE families' basis conversion helpers:

- chebyshev:  poly2cheb / cheb2poly
- hermite:    poly2herm / herm2poly
- laguerre:   poly2lag  / lag2poly
- legendre:   poly2leg  / leg2poly

Each wrapper imports numpy at call time and forwards to the
appropriate numpy.polynomial.<family>.<helper>, preserving NumPy's
dtype, ValueError, and tuple-return contracts byte-for-byte. Each is
registered alongside the family's existing module entries (next to
chebtrim, hermint, lagint, legint respectively).

Conformance test polynomial_basis_conversion_helpers_match_numpy
iterates the four (family, poly2X, X2poly) tuples and for each one:

- Calls poly2X on a power-basis vector [1, -2, 0.5, 3] and compares
  to numpy.polynomial.<family>.poly2X.
- Calls X2poly on a basis-coefficient vector [0.25, -1, 2, 0.5] and
  compares to numpy's X2poly.
- Verifies the round-trip X2poly(poly2X(power_coeffs)) matches NumPy
  to within the assert_array_matches_numpy tolerance.
- Calls poly2X on a 2-D ndarray and asserts the resulting Python
  exception type matches NumPy's (verifies the error surface, not just
  the success path).

Skips quietly when numpy is unavailable, matching the existing
polynomial test pattern.

Bead bookkeeping: marks nys5 in_progress with assignee cod-numpy so
the conversion-helper portion of the polynomial parity sweep is
tracked while the orthogonal Vandermonde follow-up (ur9y) stays open.
